Scenario.overview: Energy Simple A skilled threat actor named "Cutting Edge" launches a phishing campaign targeting an energy sector organization to deploy malware and manipulate industrial control systems, leading to operational disruptions.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: A targeted phishing email containing a malicious Excel macro attachment is sent to employees in the energy organization. The macro exploits user interaction to install malware, granting the attacker initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker leverages a known unpatched vulnerability in a legacy SCADA system (e.g., CVE-2023-XXXX) to execute arbitrary code remotely, escalating privileges within the compromised network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Malware alters Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) and Remote Terminal Unit (RTU) configurations in the energy infrastructure, causing grid instability or forced shutdowns to disrupt critical operations.

Scenario.summary: The "Cutting Edge" campaign exploited phishing and unpatched SCADA vulnerabilities to manipulate industrial control systems in the energy sector, resulting in operational disruptions through malware-induced grid instability.
